bull.b: Micron's very large trailing‑12‑month operating and free cash flows provide durable internal funding for fabs, R&D and customer commitments. Strong cash conversion and low leverage expand strategic optionality, support capex funding and capital returns, and bolster resilience across memory cycles.

bull.t: Strategic Customer Agreements And Revenue Visibility
bull.b: The portfolio of SCAs and large RPOs creates multi‑year contracted volume and priority access to capacity, materially improving revenue visibility versus pure spot sales. Contracted minima and deposits underpin production planning, reduce spot exposure, and enable more predictable investment and capacity allocation dec

bull.t: Technology & Product Leadership (HBM, DRAM, NAND Ramps)
bull.b: Successful ramps of advanced DRAM, G9 NAND and accelerated HBM4 adoption demonstrate node leadership and faster yield maturation. This supports a higher‑value product mix into AI and data center markets, strengthening long‑term differentiation and sustainable ASP advantages versus commodity peers.

bear.t: High And Rising Capital & Operating Intensity
bear.b: Micron's very large and growing capex program materially raises capital intensity and execution risk. Sustained high investment extends payback periods, makes returns sensitive to cycle normalization, and increases the consequences of any demand shortfall or ramp delays for margins and long‑term ROIC.

bear.t: Persistent Supply Constraints And Limited Line Of Sight
bear.b: Ongoing multi‑year supply tightness driven by long fab lead times, workforce and energy constraints limits bit growth and capacity flexibility. Prolonged constraints sustain price volatility, complicate customer planning and allocation, and reduce Micron's ability to reliably scale volumes and revenue predictability ov

bear.t: SCA Pricing Uncertainty And Floor Exposure
bear.b: A large share of SCA volume remains subject to floor/ceiling bands or market pricing, leaving realized revenue and margins exposed to future price moves. That weakens the revenue predictability offered by RPOs and complicates medium‑term earnings modeling even as contracted volumes increase.
